title: How to Audit Your Moving Quote: 5 Red Flags That Signal Hidden Fees
excerpt: Moving quote audit: five red flags for hidden fees, vague estimates, missing inventory, stair traps, insurance gaps, and how to vet Addis Ababa mover quotes.
content:
How to audit your moving quote: 5 red flags that signal hidden fees

A moving quote is a contract preview—if you read it like marketing, hidden fees win. Auditing estimates before you book takes twenty minutes and saves thousands. Here are five red flags that signal surcharge games in Addis Ababa mover quotes—and what to demand instead.

Red flag 1: No inventory or site visit

Phone quotes for whole apartments without room lists or photos invite "we did not know about the fourth bedroom" on invoice day. Professional quotes tie price to documented scope.

Red flag 2: Vague hourly terms

"Around 4,000 birr per hour" without crew size, minimum hours, truck class, or materials inclusion is a blank check. Demand crew count, minimum billing, and what stops the clock.

Red flag 3: Stairs, shuttle, and standby omitted

Walk-ups, narrow Atlas lanes, and elevator waits are normal in Addis—not exceptional surprises. If stairs and standby are absent from exclusions/inclusions, assume they will appear later.

Red flag 4: Insurance hand-waving

"We are insured" without certificate, declared value process, or exclusion list means you are self-insuring. Red flag when high-value items are on the truck.

Red flag 5: Large deposit, no cancellation terms

Heavy upfront cash with vague refund rules correlates with no-show or bait-and-switch trucks. Prefer structured deposits tied to dated confirmations and plate numbers.

Audit checklist before you sign

  • Written inventory or room list attached.
  • Line items: labor, truck, materials, stairs, shuttle, standby.
  • Insurance certificate or broker contact.
  • Payment schedule and cancellation clause.
  • Named crew lead and truck plate before day-of.

What good quotes look like

Transparent movers explain what changes price and how change orders work when you add a piano Friday night. Clarity is a feature, not bureaucracy.

Conclusion

Audit every moving quote for inventory discipline, hourly clarity, access surcharges, insurance paper, and fair deposits. Five red flags caught early beat one invoice argument late—hire movers who welcome scrutiny, not those who dodge it.
author: Active Movers Team
